workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_tiqora/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in the configured communication_language.
Write ALL output documents in document_language — optimized for AI agent consumption.
Every architectural claim must cite a specific file path. Never speculate.
Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps.

step.action: Load project config from {project_config} (required). Warn but continue if missing.
Load profile config from {profile_config} when available (optional).
Check for {workspace_config}. If found, parse ALL repos from the `workspace.repos` list.
Determine the list of repos to analyze: - If workspace_config found: {{repos_to_analyze}} = all repos listed under `workspace.repos` - Otherwise: {{repos_to_analyze}} = [current repo (project-root)]
step.critical: MULTI-REPO COMMIT: You MUST analyze ALL repos in {{repos_to_analyze}}, not just the cwd. Output this numbered list now: "Repos détectés (N total) : 1. {name} — {localPath} 2. {name} — {localPath} ..." You are required to complete Steps 2 and 3 for EVERY repo in this list.

step.goal: Discovery — analyse architecturale de chaque repo (lecture seule, aucun fichier écrit)
step.critical: THIS STEP IS READ-ONLY. Do NOT write any files. Collect findings in memory only.
Load Architect persona from {project-root}/_tiqora/agents/architect.md. Apply architecture-scan reflex.

action.action: 2.1 — Entry Points: identify main entry files (main.ts, index.ts, app.py, etc.) in {{current_repo_path}}.
2.2 — Tech Stack: read dependency manifests (package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, go.mod, pom.xml).
2.3 — Directory Structure: map top-level dirs with one-sentence role descriptions. Go one level deeper for core dirs.
2.4 — Architectural Patterns: monolith/microservices, MVC/hexagonal/clean, state management, API style, testing strategy.
2.5 — Key Interfaces and Contracts: shared types, API contracts. Cite exact file paths.
2.6 — Tech Debt: flag items as [CRITICAL], [MODERATE], or [MINOR] with file reference.
Store findings as {{findings_repo_N}} in memory. Do not write to disk.

check.action: 2.7 — Inter-repo: map cross-repo contracts, shared packages, event/message boundaries.

step.output: [GENERATE-CONTEXT] ✓ Analyse complète | {N} repo(s) analysé(s) — en attente de validation

step.critical: STOP HERE. Do NOT proceed to file generation until the user explicitly confirms.
Only execute this step if the user has confirmed. If they provided corrections, incorporate them.
REPO-AWARE: each architecture document goes INTO the repo it describes.

step.action: 5.1 — Project Purpose: 2–3 sentences. Sources: README, .tiqora.yaml, docs/.
5.2 — Current Sprint: name/number, goal, end date. Sources: .tiqora/sprints/, .tiqora/context/, README.
5.3 — Active and High-Priority Tickets: status in_progress or todo + high priority.
5.4 — Epics Overview: 1 sentence per active epic.
5.5 — Blockers: tickets marked blocked with dependency chain.
Load both {architecture_output} and {pm_output} in full before writing.

step.critical: Each repo gets its OWN architecture context — not the global synthesis.
step.ask: Contexte généré. Voulez-vous distribuer vers les fichiers de config agents ? - (A) `CLAUDE.md` dans chaque repo (Claude Code) - (B) `.cursorrules` dans chaque repo (Cursor) - (C) `llms.txt` dans chaque repo (multi-agent standard) - (D) Les trois - (N) Non, garder dans `.tiqora/context/` uniquement

check.if: user chooses A, B, C, or D
check.action: For each repo in {{repos_to_analyze}}: - Load {{current_repo_path}}/.tiqora/context/architecture.md - For each selected target: read existing file, upsert "## Tiqora Architecture Context" section - Write file
Also distribute global-context.md to primary repo under "## Tiqora Global Context".

check.output: [GENERATE-CONTEXT] ✓ Distribué vers : {{list of files written per repo}}
